summary: A small, resource-poor Hashemite monarchy that has survived by positioning itself as a stable US ally and mediator in the Middle East, hosting millions of Palestinian and Syrian refugees while maintaining a cold peace with Israel.

history.detail: Britain installs Emir Abdullah I; artificial state carved from Ottoman remains.
Jordan occupies West Bank; 700,000 Palestinian refugees flee to Jordan.
Six-Day War; Israel takes West Bank and East Jerusalem from Jordan.
Second Arab country to normalise; King Hussein signs Wadi Araba treaty.
Protests suppressed; limited reforms; King Abdullah retains control.

key_doctrines: Hashemite custodianship of Jerusalem holy sites

resources.water: One of world's most water-scarce countries; Dead Sea declining
resources.agriculture: Potash and phosphate mining; vegetables, olives

follow_the_money.detail: Jordan receives ~$1.5B/yr US aid; largest per-capita recipient in region
World's 3rd largest phosphate reserves; major export

connections.detail: Peace treaty; water for electricity deals; security cooperation
Custodianship of Jerusalem holy sites; many Palestinians hold Jordanian passports
Non-NATO major ally; US bases and training
